critique.works_well: The dark, high-contrast aesthetic reads as technical and modern — not a template. Visual consistency holds all the way down the page without collapsing mid-scroll, which signals an execution-capable team. Trust signals (logos, use cases, structured feature sections) are present and add real credibility when you get past the fold.
critique.looks_weak: The trust signals are buried below the fold — the hero gives no immediate proof of traction, making the first 30 seconds feel weaker than the actual product deserves. The CTA lacks urgency or differentiation; it reads as generic without a compelling reason to act immediately.
critique.single_biggest_improvement: Pull one or two recognizable customer logos up into the hero section — the credibility is there, it just needs to be visible within the first 5 seconds.
